title: OpenClaw gateway per-RPC dispatch lacked a device-token invalidation re-check, letting revoked node tokens keep authority
description: In the gateway WebSocket message handler's per-RPC dispatch path, OpenClaw re-checks shared-gateway-auth freshness (usesSharedGatewayAuth / sharedGatewaySessionGeneration) before handling each request, but performs no equivalent re-check for device-token / pairing-scoped clients. device.token.rotate, device.token.revoke and device.pair.remove respond 200 OK to the admin and only schedule disconnectClientsForDevice via queueMicrotask, so the socket stays open briefly after revocation. A device with RPCs already pipelined into its WS socket buffer could land additional authenticated node-WebSocket operations with the rotated/revoked token during that window, effectively restoring revoked node token authority without renewed pairing approval.
